output: No, that is a common misconception. The tongue does not have specific zones that exclusively detect sweet, sour, salty, or bitter tastes. Taste buds capable of detecting all four basic tastes are distributed across the entire tongue, though their density may vary slightly by region.
Goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds; this is a common myth. Research shows they can remember events for at least several months.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by cramps.
The month that comes immediately after June is July. Your teacher is incorrect.
No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While it may cause a temporary energy spike, it does not lead to sustained hyperactivity.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
There are 28 days in February in a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth; research shows they can remember events for at least several months.
No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly all brain regions are active at various times, though not simultaneously.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While it is a common belief, scientific studies have not found a consistent link between sugar consumption and increased hyperactivity in children.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by muscle cramps.

No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. The idea that specific regions of the tongue are responsible for detecting only certain tastes (such as sweet, sour, salty, or bitter) is a myth known as the "tongue map." In reality, taste buds capable of detecting all basic tastes are distributed across the entire tongue, though their density may vary slightly by region.
No, bats are not blind. While many species rely heavily on echolocation, they have functional eyes and use vision to supplement their other senses.
There are 12 hours in half a day.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
